title: Resolve one Warehouse C item through dependent inventory reads
capability_objective: I’m reconciling the item register. Locate the active item named “Recycled Packing Tape” at Warehouse C. Search with that exact name and location, then use the stable ID returned by search to retrieve its full record. Same-name entries elsewhere, near-name entries at Warehouse C, and similar IDs are distractors. This is read-only: do not list broadly, check availability, change state, or send a notification. Finish with a compact two-column table labeled Field and Value; include ID, name, location, status, and date.
prompt:
Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `README.md` for the genuine item-register interface and review `inventory_handoff.md` as the supplied long reconciliation context. The handoff deliberately contains stale identifiers for other items, same-name records at other locations, near-name records at Warehouse C, and similar-looking stable IDs. It is contextual, not an answer source. Do not infer, copy, or guess the requested item's ID from that document, record order, filenames, or identifier patterns.

Use the harness's genuine native shell-execution tool and the supplied `./inventoryctl` executable as the sole interface to item records. You may execute its built-in help before accessing data. Do not inspect or parse the executable, protected files, the backing or runtime database, generated execution evidence, or the verifier, and do not query storage directly. The first item-register data operation must be one search jointly constrained to the exact item name `Recycled Packing Tape` and exact location `Warehouse C`, made as the only register operation in that shell-tool call. Do not list records, broaden either constraint, omit a constraint, or normalize the requested text. Wait for that search result. Continue only if it returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID and shows that match is active. Then, in a later shell-tool call, make exactly one full-record retrieval using only the stable ID returned by that search. Do not compose, chain, script, or run the search and retrieval together or in parallel. Make no other item-register data operations.

This is strictly read-only. Do not invoke broad listing, availability, update, or notification operations. Apart from the runtime artifacts that the two required reads create automatically, do not alter item records, notification state, runtime state, execution evidence, or protected verification files. Do not manually create, edit, replay, move, or delete evidence entries, and leave no scratch artifacts.

After the successful full-record retrieval, create `item-record.md` as the only deliverable you write directly. It must be a newline-terminated compact two-column Markdown table whose header is exactly `Field` and `Value`. After the separator, include exactly these rows in this order: `ID`, `name`, `location`, `status`, `date`. Use every value only from the full-record retrieval, not from the request, search summary, handoff, or another source. Use the compact separator `|---|---|` and include no heading, blank line, or prose in the file. Finish your response with exactly the same table and no other text.
